# terraform-module-gcp-postgres-management

 [![Latest Release](https://img.shields.io/github/release/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-postgres-management.svg?style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-postgres-management/releases/latest) [![Last Updated](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-postgres-management.svg?style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-postgres-management/commits)


Terraform module that manages PostgreSQL users, roles, databases, and credentials on Google Cloud Platform. It delegates all PostgreSQL engine operations to [terraform-module-postgres-management](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-postgres-management) and handles the GCP-specific concerns: connecting through Cloud SQL Auth Proxy or a direct host, storing per-user credentials as versioned secrets in GCP Secret Manager (with optional CMEK encryption via Cloud KMS), and emitting structured Hoop enterprise connection output for each user.


---

This project is part of our comprehensive approach towards DevOps Acceleration.

It's 100% Open Source and licensed under the [APACHE2](LICENSE).







We have [*lots of terraform modules*][terraform_modules] that are Open Source and we are trying to get them well-maintained!. Check them out!






## Introduction

This module is the GCP adapter layer for PostgreSQL database management within the CloudOps Works Terraform ecosystem. It covers three distinct responsibilities:

**1. Connection source — Cloud SQL or direct host**

When `cloudsql.enabled = true` the module connects through the Cloud SQL Auth Proxy, resolving the instance connection string automatically from `cloudsql.instance_name`. Admin credentials can be supplied inline or read from an existing GCP Secret Manager secret (`cloudsql.from_secret`). When `cloudsql.enabled = false` the module accepts a `direct` connection block with an explicit host, port, username and password, which is useful for non-Cloud-SQL PostgreSQL servers running on GCE, GKE, or anywhere reachable from the Terraform runner.

**2. Credential storage — GCP Secret Manager**

Every user managed by the module gets a dedicated, versioned secret in GCP Secret Manager. The secret payload is a JSON object containing `host`, `port`, `username`, `password`, `dbname`, `engine`, `sslmode`, and `connection_string` keys. Secrets are labeled with org and environment tags and can be encrypted with a customer-managed Cloud KMS key (`secrets_kms_key_name`). Password rotation is controlled by `password_rotation_period` (days) and `force_reset`.

**3. Hoop integration**

When `hoop.enabled = true` the module produces a `hoop_connections` output that follows the CloudOps Works Hoop connection schema. In community mode (`hoop.community = true`) each user's connection block contains a `null` value (the credential is already in GCP Secret Manager). In enterprise mode (`hoop.community = false`) the block embeds the full connection string and optional access-control groups, ready to be consumed by the Hoop gateway configuration.

**Delegation to base module**

All PostgreSQL DDL operations (CREATE DATABASE, CREATE ROLE, GRANT) are performed by the upstream [`terraform-module-postgres-management`](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-postgres-management) module, which is sourced via `module "db"` in `module-db.tf`. This module pins the upstream source at a specific tag and passes through the `databases`, `users`, `roles`, `password_rotation_period`, and `force_reset` variables unchanged.

## Usage


**IMPORTANT:** The `master` branch is used in `source` just as an example. In your code, do not pin to `master` because there may be breaking changes between releases.
Instead pin to the release tag (e.g. `?ref=vX.Y.Z`) of one of our [latest releases](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-postgres-management/releases).


Add the module as a Terragrunt dependency and configure it through `.boilerplate/inputs.yaml` (or the equivalent `locals.local_vars` map in your `terragrunt.hcl`).

```hcl
# terragrunt.hcl — minimal wiring (boilerplate pattern)
terraform {
  source = "git::https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-postgres-management.git?ref=v1.1.1"
}

locals {
  local_vars = yamldecode(file("${get_terragrunt_dir()}/.boilerplate/inputs.yaml"))
}

inputs = merge(
  local.local_vars,
  {
    org = local.org          # injected by the parent hierarchy
    extra_tags = local.tags  # injected by the parent hierarchy
  }
)
```

**Full `.boilerplate/inputs.yaml` reference**

```yaml
# ── Connection source ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

cloudsql:                            # (Optional) Cloud SQL instance connection settings.
  enabled: true                      # (Required within block) Use Cloud SQL Auth Proxy. Default: false.
  instance_name: "my-project:us-central1:my-pg-instance"
                                     # (Required when enabled) Cloud SQL instance connection name.
  project_id: "my-gcp-project"       # (Optional) Override GCP project. Default: current project.
  from_secret: false                 # (Optional) Read admin credentials from Secret Manager. Default: false.
  secret_id: "pg-admin-credentials"  # (Required when from_secret=true) Secret Manager secret ID.
                                     #   The secret must be a JSON object with keys:
                                     #   host, port, username, password, dbname.
  server_name: "my-pg"               # (Optional) Override server_name used in secret paths and hoop output.
  sslmode: "require"                 # (Optional) SSL mode. Default: require.
  superuser: false                   # (Optional) Is admin user a superuser. Default: false.

direct:                              # (Optional) Direct host connection — used when cloudsql.enabled=false.
  server_name: "pg-prod"             # (Required) Identifier used in secret paths and hoop output.
  host: "10.0.0.5"                   # (Required) PostgreSQL host.
  port: 5432                         # (Required) PostgreSQL port.
  username: "postgres"               # (Required) Admin username.
  password: "s3cr3t"                 # (Required) Admin password.
  engine: "postgresql"               # (Optional) Engine name stored in credentials. Default: postgresql.
  db_name: "postgres"                # (Required) Default database for provider connection.
  sslmode: "require"                 # (Optional) SSL mode. Default: require.
  superuser: false                   # (Optional) Is admin user a superuser. Default: false.

# ── Databases ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

databases:                           # (Optional) Map of PostgreSQL databases to create.
  app_db:
    name: "appdb"                    # (Required) Name of the database.
    create_owner: true               # (Optional) Create a dedicated owner role (<name>_ow). Default: false.
    owner: ""                        # (Optional) Owner name when create_owner=false.
    collate: "en_US.UTF-8"           # (Optional) Collation. Default: en_US.UTF-8.
    ctype: "en_US.UTF-8"             # (Optional) Character type. Default: en_US.UTF-8.
    connection_limit: -1             # (Optional) Max connections. Default: -1 (unlimited).
    is_template: false               # (Optional) Mark as template. Default: false.
    template: "template0"            # (Optional) Source template. Default: template0.
    encoding: "UTF8"                 # (Optional) Encoding. Default: UTF8.
    allow_connections: true          # (Optional) Allow connections. Default: true.
    alter_object_ownership: false    # (Optional) Reassign objects to owner on create. Default: false.
    import: false                    # (Optional) Import existing database (skip CREATE). Default: false.
    schemas:
      - name: "public"               # (Required) Schema name.
        owner: "appdb_ow"            # (Optional) Schema owner.
        reuse: true                  # (Optional) Use IF NOT EXISTS. Default: true.
        cascade_on_delete: false     # (Optional) DROP CASCADE on destroy. Default: false.

# ── Users ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

users:                               # (Optional) Map of PostgreSQL users to create and manage.
  app_user:
    name: "appuser"                  # (Required) PostgreSQL username.
    grant: "readwrite"               # (Required) Grant type: owner | readwrite | readonly.
    db_ref: "app_db"                 # (Optional) Reference to a databases key; takes precedence over database_name.
    database_name: ""                # (Optional) Database name when db_ref not set.
    database_owner: ""               # (Conditionally Required) Owner role when grant=owner without db_ref.
    schema: "public"                 # (Optional) Schema to grant on. Default: public.
    login: true                      # (Optional) Can login. Default: true.
    superuser: false                 # (Optional) Is superuser. Default: false.
    create_database: false           # (Optional) Can create databases. Default: false.
    replication: false               # (Optional) Can replicate. Default: false.
    encrypted_password: true         # (Optional) Encrypt password. Default: true.
    inherit: true                    # (Optional) Inherit role privileges. Default: true.
    create_role: false               # (Optional) Can create roles. Default: false.
    connection_limit: -1             # (Optional) Max connections. Default: -1 (unlimited).
    import: false                    # (Optional) Import existing user (skip CREATE). Default: false.
    connection_string_type: ""       # (Optional) Format for connection_string in secret.
                                     #   Values: jdbc | dotnet | odbc | gopq | node-pg | psycopg | rustpg.
    hoop:
      access_control: []             # (Optional) Access control groups for this user's hoop connection.

# ── Roles ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

roles:                               # (Optional) Map of PostgreSQL roles (no-login) to create.
  readonly_role:
    name: "readonly"                 # (Required) Role name.
    grant: "readonly"                # (Required) Grant type: owner | readwrite | readonly.
    db_ref: "app_db"                 # (Optional) Reference to a databases key.
    database_name: ""                # (Optional) Database name when db_ref not set.
    schema: "public"                 # (Optional) Schema. Default: public.
    create_database: false           # (Optional) Default: false.
    replication: false               # (Optional) Default: false.
    inherit: true                    # (Optional) Default: true.
    create_role: false               # (Optional) Default: false.
    connection_limit: -1             # (Optional) Default: -1.
    import: false                    # (Optional) Import existing role. Default: false.

# ── Hoop integration ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

hoop:                                # (Optional) Hoop enterprise connection output settings.
  enabled: false                     # (Optional) Emit hoop_connections output. Default: false.
  agent_id: "xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx"
                                     # (Required when enabled + enterprise) Hoop agent UUID.
  community: true                    # (Optional) true=community (null payload, secret in GCP SM);
                                     #   false=enterprise (embedded connection string). Default: true.
  import: false                      # (Optional) Import existing hoop connection. Default: false.
  default_sslmode: "require"         # (Optional) SSL mode in hoop secrets. Default: require.
  tags: {}                           # (Optional) Tags map to attach to all hoop connections.
  access_control: []                 # (Optional) Default access control groups for all connections.

# ── Secret Manager settings ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

secrets_kms_key_name: null           # (Optional) Full resource name of a Cloud KMS key used to encrypt
                                     #   secrets at rest (CMEK). Default: null (Google-managed key).
                                     #   Format: projects/<p>/locations/<l>/keyRings/<r>/cryptoKeys/<k>

# ── Password lifecycle ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

password_rotation_period: 90         # (Optional) Password rotation period in days. Default: 90.
force_reset: false                   # (Optional) Force reset all passwords on next apply. Default: false.
```

## Quick Start

1. **Add the source** to your Terragrunt stack:

   ```hcl
   # terragrunt.hcl
   terraform {
     source = "git::https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-postgres-management.git?ref=v1.1.1"
   }
   ```

2. **Copy the inputs template** to your environment directory:

   ```sh
   mkdir -p .boilerplate
   curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-postgres-management/master/.boilerplate/inputs.yaml \
     > .boilerplate/inputs.yaml
   ```

3. **Configure the connection source.** For Cloud SQL:

   ```yaml
   cloudsql:
     enabled: true
     instance_name: "my-project:us-central1:my-instance"
     server_name: "my-instance"
   ```

   Or for a direct host:

   ```yaml
   direct:
     server_name: "pg-dev"
     host: "127.0.0.1"
     port: 5432
     username: "postgres"
     password: "postgres"
     db_name: "postgres"
   ```

4. **Define at least one database and one user:**

   ```yaml
   databases:
     mydb:
       name: "mydb"
       create_owner: true

   users:
     app:
       name: "app"
       grant: "readwrite"
       db_ref: "mydb"
   ```

5. **Apply:**

   ```sh
   terragrunt apply
   ```

   The user's credentials will be available as a versioned secret in GCP Secret Manager under a path derived from `server_name` and the username.


## Examples

### Cloud SQL with Secret Manager credentials and Hoop enterprise output

```yaml
# .boilerplate/inputs.yaml

cloudsql:
  enabled: true
  instance_name: "my-project:us-central1:pg-prod"
  from_secret: true
  secret_id: "pg-admin-creds"
  server_name: "pg-prod"
  sslmode: "require"

databases:
  orders:
    name: "orders"
    create_owner: true

users:
  api_user:
    name: "api"
    grant: "readwrite"
    db_ref: "orders"
    connection_string_type: "jdbc"
    hoop:
      access_control: ["engineering"]

hoop:
  enabled: true
  community: false
  agent_id: "aaaaaaaa-bbbb-cccc-dddd-eeeeeeeeeeee"
  access_control: ["dba-team"]

secrets_kms_key_name: "projects/my-project/locations/us-central1/keyRings/terraform/cryptoKeys/secrets"
password_rotation_period: 30
force_reset: false
```

### Direct connection (non-Cloud-SQL PostgreSQL)

```yaml
# .boilerplate/inputs.yaml

direct:
  server_name: "pg-analytics"
  host: "10.128.0.10"
  port: 5432
  username: "postgres"
  password: "change-me"
  db_name: "postgres"
  sslmode: "disable"

databases:
  analytics:
    name: "analytics"
    create_owner: true

users:
  etl_user:
    name: "etl"
    grant: "readwrite"
    db_ref: "analytics"

hoop:
  enabled: false

password_rotation_period: 90
force_reset: false
```

## Inputs

| Name | Description | Type | Default | Required |
|------|-------------|------|---------|:--------:|
| <a name="input_cloudsql"></a> [cloudsql](#input\_cloudsql) | Cloud SQL instance connection attributes — see inline docs. | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_databases"></a> [databases](#input\_databases) | Map of PostgreSQL databases — see inline docs for full YAML structure. | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_direct"></a> [direct](#input\_direct) | Direct connection attributes — see inline docs. | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_extra_tags"></a> [extra\_tags](#input\_extra\_tags) | Extra tags to add to the resources | `map(string)` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_force_reset"></a> [force\_reset](#input\_force\_reset) | (Optional) Force reset all passwords. Default: false. | `bool` | `false` | no |
| <a name="input_hoop"></a> [hoop](#input\_hoop) | Hoop connection settings — see inline docs. | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_is_hub"></a> [is\_hub](#input\_is\_hub) | Is this a hub or spoke configuration? | `bool` | `false` | no |
| <a name="input_org"></a> [org](#input\_org) | Organization details | <pre>object({<br/>    organization_name = string<br/>    organization_unit = string<br/>    environment_type  = string<br/>    environment_name  = string<br/>  })</pre> | n/a | yes |
| <a name="input_password_rotation_period"></a> [password\_rotation\_period](#input\_password\_rotation\_period) | (Optional) Password rotation period in days. Default: 90. | `number` | `90` | no |
| <a name="input_roles"></a> [roles](#input\_roles) | Map of PostgreSQL roles (no-login) — see inline docs for full YAML structure. | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_secrets_kms_key_name"></a> [secrets\_kms\_key\_name](#input\_secrets\_kms\_key\_name) | (Optional) GCP KMS key name to encrypt secrets at rest. | `string` | `null` | no |
| <a name="input_spoke_def"></a> [spoke\_def](#input\_spoke\_def) | Spoke ID Number, must be a 3 digit number | `string` | `"001"` | no |
| <a name="input_users"></a> [users](#input\_users) | Map of PostgreSQL users — see inline docs for full YAML structure. | `any` | `{}` | no |

## Outputs

| Name | Description |
|------|-------------|
| <a name="output_databases"></a> [databases](#output\_databases) | Map of created PostgreSQL databases. |
| <a name="output_hoop_connections"></a> [hoop\_connections](#output\_hoop\_connections) | Hoop connection definitions (enterprise only; community returns null — GCP SM has no sub-key access). |
| <a name="output_users"></a> [users](#output\_users) | Map of created PostgreSQL users with their GCP Secret Manager secret IDs. |
